thread.title: Maharashtra Congress chief Nana Patole wins Sakoli Assembly seat by 208 votes
thread.title_full: Maharashtra Congress chief Nana Patole wins Sakoli Assembly seat by 208 votes

author: @indiatoday
title: Maharashtra Congress chief Nana Patole wins Sakoli Assembly seat by 208 votes
text:
Maharashtra Congress chief Nana Patole wins Sakoli Assembly seat by 208 votes
While Nana Patole got 96,795 votes, his closest rival, BJP's Avinash Brahmankar, received 96,587 votes.
In Short
- Nana Patole retained Sakoli seat by winning 96,795 votes
- BJP's Avinash Brahmankar was second with 96,587 votes
- Mahayuti won 233 seats in Maharashtra, MVA settled for 49
Maharashtra Congress chief Nana Patole on Saturday barely managed to win the Sakoli Assembly seat by a margin as narrow as 208 votes. He defeated his nearest rival, BJP's Avinash Brahmankar, to retain the Sakoli seat.
While Patole polled 96,795 votes, Brahmankar breathed under his neck with 96,587 votes. Independent candidate Somadatta Karanjekar, who won 18,309 votes, was a distant third.
Vanchit Bahujan Aaghadi's Dr Avinash Nanhe was fourth with 11,188 votes.
The [Maha Vikas Aghadi](https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/assembly/story/uddhav-thackeray-maharashtra-nda-win-mahayuti-mahavikas-aghadi-eknath-shinde-shiv-sena-bjp-2638928-2024-11-23), led by Congress, was humbled in the Maharashtra Assembly elections by the [ruling Mahayuti alliance](https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/assembly/story/devendra-fadnavis-shares-his-viral-2019-speech-maharashtra-election-victory-bjp-mahayuti-2638962-2024-11-23) of the BJP, Shiv Sena (Eknath Shinde) and Nationalist Congress Party (Ajit Pawar).
The [elections results announced by the Election Commission](https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/assembly/story/maharashtra-jharkhand-assembly-election-results-counting-of-votes-latest-updates-mahayuti-mva-bjp-jmm-congress-2638056-2024-11-23) on Saturday showed that the ruling alliance won 233 seats, while the Mahayuti could win only 49.
The 288-member Maharashtra Legislative Assembly went to polls in a single phase on November 20.
